title: Layia gaillardioides
intro: Layia gaillardioides, also known as the tidytip or tidy tips, is a small annual plant belonging to the daisy family (Asteraceae) that is native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its potential health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can help to alleviate symptoms of colds and flu. The bioactive constituents of Layia gaillardioides include sesquiterpene lactones and flavonoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Layia gaillardioides is commonly prepared as a tincture or infused oil, and is often used to make a cough syrup or a salve for topical application to soothe irritated skin and mucous membranes.
taxonomy: Layia gaillardioides is a species of flowering plant in the daisy family, Asteraceae. It is native to the western coast of North America, ranging from California to Oregon. The plant belongs to the genus Layia, which is a small group of annual or perennial plants. Layia gaillardioides is further classified under the subfamily Asteroideae and tribe Madieae. The plant's classification places it within the larger group of flowering plants, the angiosperms.

The plant is believed to have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may help to soothe skin irritations and promote wound healing.
